meta.title: Casale La Cocca · Exclusive luxury villa · Itria Valley, Puglia
meta.description: A stone farmhouse in the Itria Valley, entirely yours. 5 en-suite suites, saltwater infinity pool, exclusive use. Between Cisternino and Ostuni, 20 minutes from the sea.

hero.title: Casale La Cocca
hero.subtitle: Five suites, a pool on the horizon, a 1950s stone farmhouse. Just you, for as long as you want to stay.
hero.location: In the countryside between Cisternino, Ostuni and Martina Franca
hero.imageAlt: The infinity pool at Casale La Cocca at dusk, with the stone farmhouse beyond

overview.title: A farmhouse that teaches you to slow down
overview.body: Set among the olive groves of Cisternino, restored by local craftsmen who kept everything: the limestone vaults, the traditional tiled floors, the walls that hold the cool of summer. Outside, a 14-metre infinity pool. Inside, five en-suite suites and spaces that ask nothing, except that you stay.

pullquote.text: True luxury makes no noise. It makes light.

gallery.title: Stone, light, water
gallery.subtitle: Author photography of the spaces and the light that moves through them.

spaces.title: Four ways to live the villa
spaces.subtitle: The main stone house and three independent suites, each with its own en-suite bathroom, each with its own character.

casale.name: Main house
casale.body: The heart of the villa: stone vaults, a living room with fireplace, an open kitchen with original arches. Two master en-suite suites. The sitting room opens directly onto pool and garden: inside and outside blur together.

poolSuite.name: Pool Suite
poolSuite.body: Waking up with water in front of your eyes. Wide glass doors open onto the pool and garden: the distance from bed to water is measured in seconds. En-suite bathroom.

gardenSuite.name: Garden Suite
gardenSuite.body: Doors onto the green, light filtering through the olive trees. A ground-floor suite with direct access to the garden. Puglia comes into the room. En-suite bathroom.

firstFloorSuite.name: First-floor suite
firstFloorSuite.body: A private external staircase, a terrace entirely your own. From above: the outline of the olive trees and the blue of the pool. En-suite bathroom. You come here when you want to be found.

outdoors.title: Water, olive trees, sky
outdoors.body: The 14×4-metre saltwater infinity pool has no edge, it has a horizon. Around it: a sun deck with loungers, a fully equipped outdoor kitchen for sunset dinners, a gas barbecue, and centuries-old olive trees that cast shade without asking permission.

amenities.title: Everything you need, without asking
amenities.subtitle: Every detail chosen so the only thing on your mind is time.
amenities.note: Video surveillance is switched off during your stay.

location.title: Three hill towns. A walking path. The sea in 20 minutes.
location.body: 400 metres from the Apulian Aqueduct walking and cycling path. Three whitewashed hill towns within 10 km. The sea 20 minutes away. Brindisi and Bari airports within the hour.
location.mapAlt: Map of Casale La Cocca's location in the Itria Valley, between Cisternino and Ostuni

contact.title: Got the dates in mind?
contact.subtitle: Tell us when you'd like to come. We'll reply within 24 hours with availability and pricing, no commitment needed.

t1.text: "A week suspended in time. The infinity pool, the stone, the silence, everything speaks of a luxury that doesn't shout."
t1.author: Family M., Milan · summer 2024

t2.text: "We celebrated a milestone birthday here. The outdoor kitchen, the barbecue, the pool at sunset: it was perfect."
t2.author: Group of friends, Rome · July 2024

t3.text: "The location is incredible: total quiet, but Martina Franca is ten minutes away. We'll be back."
t3.author: Couple, London · September 2024

testimonials.disclaimer: Reviews collected from booking platforms. Names have been abbreviated for privacy.

footer.tagline: Stone, olive trees, water. Itria Valley, Puglia.
